agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Critical race condition between stock check and decrement will oversell inventory under concurrent requests"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"TOCTOU Race Condition: Stock Check vs. Decrement","risk":"Multiple concurrent requests read the same stock value, all pass the check, then all decrement — selling more units than exist. With popular products on a public API, this WILL happen.","severity":"Critical","location":"addToCart() lines 5-9 (getStock → check → addItem → decrementStock)","reproduction":"Product has 1 unit in stock. Two users call addToCart simultaneously. Both read stock=1, both pass the check, both add to cart, both decrement. Stock goes to -1. Two users bought 1 item that exists once.","fix":"Use an atomic compare-and-decrement operation or a database transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { const stock = await tx.products.getStockForUpdate(productId); if (stock < quantity) throw new Error('Insufficient stock'); await tx.products.decrementStock(productId, quantity); await tx.cart.addItem(userId, productId, quantity); });`","time_to_fix":"1-2 hours","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Inventory accuracy, overselling prevention"},{"title":"Non-Atomic Cart Add + Stock Decrement — Partial Failure Leaves Inconsistent State","risk":"If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB timeout, connection drop), the item is in the user's cart but stock was never decremented. Other users can also buy it. Conversely, if you fix the order (decrement first, then addItem), a failure on addItem means stock is lost with no cart entry.","severity":"Critical","location":"addToCart() lines 8-9","reproduction":"Call addToCart. db.cart.addItem succeeds. db.products.decrementStock throws a transient DB error. Cart now contains an item that stock doesn't account for.","fix":"Wrap both operations in a single database transaction so they commit or rollback atomically (same transaction block as finding #1).","time_to_fix":"Included in finding #1 fix","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Data consistency between cart and inventory"},{"title":"No Input Validation on Public API Inputs","risk":"quantity could be 0, negative, or a non-integer. A negative quantity would pass the stock check (stock is always >= negative number) and then INCREMENT stock via decrementStock(-n), creating inventory out of thin air. Zero quantity wastes a DB round-trip.","severity":"High","location":"addToCart() function entry — no validation on quantity, userId, or productId","reproduction":"Call addToCart('user1', 'prod1', -10). Stock check: 5 < -10 is false, passes. decrementStock('prod1', -10) adds 10 to stock. Free inventory.","fix":"Add validation at function entry: `if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity <= 0) throw new Error('Invalid quantity'); if (!userId || !productId) throw new Error('Missing required fields');`","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security, inventory integrity"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No idempotency protection","description":"If the client retries a failed/timed-out request, the same item could be added to the cart multiple times. Consider an idempotency key.","location":"addToCart() — no deduplication mechanism"},{"title":"Stock decrement should happen before cart add","description":"Even within a transaction, decrementing stock first (the scarce resource) is safer — it acts as the lock. Adding to cart is the less contentious operation.","location":"addToCart() lines 8-9 — order of operations"}],"suggestions":["Consider a database-level CHECK constraint ensuring stock >= 0 as a safety net against overselling","Add rate limiting per userId on this endpoint to mitigate bot-driven stock manipulation","Log failed stock checks for monitoring demand signals on popular products"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code will oversell inventory from day one under any meaningful concurrent traffic. The bug is not latent — it is guaranteed to manifest on popular products.","rollback_capability":"N/A — standalone function review, no deployment context","backward_compatibility":"N/A","monitoring_coverage":"No logging or metrics on stock conflicts, failed checks, or partial failures — failures will be silent until customers complain","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":false,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":[]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Fix the race condition and wrap in a transaction before shipping."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":97,"high_confidence_areas":["Race condition is textbook TOCTOU — certain under concurrency","Partial failure inconsistency is guaranteed without transactions","Negative quantity exploit is trivially reproducible"],"low_confidence_areas":["Cannot verify if db driver already provides implicit transactions (unlikely but possible)"],"assumptions":["db.products.decrementStock performs arithmetic decrement (stock = stock - quantity)","No database-level constraints prevent negative stock values","No external transaction wrapper exists around this function"]}}
